fields.description:
# Attested signing: pin SHA into the signed manifest, record verified_at_sha, claim-gate coherence

## Why

A signed verdict is only worth anything if it is reproducible and bound to specific content.
Today the completion verdict signs a manifest and stores git `head_sha` as AUDIT METADATA
ONLY (signing.py docstring) — nothing cryptographically pins WHAT code was verified. Attested
mode must resolve->pin->record->sign the immutable SHA.

## What

- **Bind the SHA through the EXISTING MANIFEST CHANNEL, not a new signed-payload field.**
  `signing._canonical_payload` signs exactly `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and
  `sign_manifest`/`compute_signature` already sign the full `manifest: list[str]`. So carry
  the pin as a MANIFEST STEP (e.g. `verified-at-sha: <sha>`): it enters the signed bytes (=>
  cryptographically bound + reproducible) WITHOUT touching `_canonical_payload` or bumping
  `PAYLOAD_VERSION` (signing.py:53-56) — therefore NO invalidation of existing certified
  closures. Do NOT add the SHA to the canonical payload schema (that path invalidates all
  prior signatures and is explicitly rejected here).
- Also record `verified_at_sha` on the ticket as a queryable field for tooling/UX; the
  TRUST anchor is the manifest step, the ticket field is convenience metadata.
- Keep the existing `head_sha` audit-metadata field AS IS (not removed/deprecated) — additive.
- SHA resolution boundary: resolve the client `ref` (default `origin/main`) to an immutable
  SHA; in attested mode an UNRESOLVABLE ref fails closed (no sign). One shared ref-resolution
  boundary is used by both this gate and the claim gate (below).
- `verified_at_sha` ticket write + the manifest pin are atomic + idempotent under retry
  (set-once per SHA; re-verifying against a DIFFERENT SHA overwrites so no stale pin persists).
- Bar signing in `local` source mode: verify + report, NO signature (the `--force-close`
  no-signature signal).
- Shape the manifest pin so a future move to DSSE + asymmetric + transparency log
  (sigstore/cosign) is an envelope swap, not a rewrite. KEEP HMAC now (single trust domain);
  limits documented in S6.
- Claim-gate coherence (ADR 0002): plan-review binds `file_impact` `{path:hash}` AT the
  pinned SHA; the claim-gate freshness re-check resolves through the SAME shared ref-resolution
  boundary (not a second hash site). Back-out: the consolidation is reversible to the prior
  per-site resolution; update ADR 0002 to record the decision.

## Scope

- In: manifest-channel SHA binding (no payload-version bump), `verified_at_sha` ticket field,
  resolution boundary + fail-closed, atomic/idempotent/overwrite pin, local-mode signing bar,
  claim-gate shared-resolution coherence + ADR 0002 update + back-out.
- Out: snapshot/cache (S1/S2); gate parameter threading (S3); HMAC-trust-model docs (S6, dependency).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An attested PASS adds a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` MANIFEST STEP that is covered by the
      signature; verifying the signature certifies the SHA. No change to `_canonical_payload`
      and NO `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump (a regression test asserts pre-existing signatures still verify).
- [ ] `verified_at_sha` is also recorded as a queryable ticket field; re-running against the
      same SHA reproduces a verdict + manifest over identical content.
- [ ] The existing `head_sha` audit field is preserved (additive, not removed).
- [ ] The pin write is atomic + idempotent under retry; re-verifying against a different SHA
      overwrites it (no stale SHA, no double-pin) — proven by a concurrency/retry test, not inspection.
- [ ] An unresolvable ref in attested mode fails closed (no signature); a `local`/dirty run
      never emits a signature.
- [ ] Plan-review `file_impact` hashing and the claim-gate freshness re-check resolve the ref
      through ONE shared boundary (same pinned-SHA basis); a negative test asserts they hash at
      the SAME ref (guards whole-HEAD divergence), not an output snapshot. ADR 0002 updated.
